popularStory.headline: Another Story about Thomas Reade
popularStory.description:
Save to my tree
Sir Knight Thomas Reade
Another story is that Sir Knight Thomas Reade was born 1 Jul 1545 in Barton Court, Berkshire, Hertfordshire, England. His parents were Sir Knight Thomas READE I …

zh.lifeSummary: William Reed於大約1587年出生在英國。他於1628年與Mabel Kendall在英國諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼波斯頓並於1647年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利且於1648年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃本。他去世於1656年10月31日在英國諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Reed于大约1587年出生在英国。他于1628年与Mabel Kendall在英国諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼波斯頓并于1647年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利且于1648年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃本。他去世于1656年10月31日在英国諾圖波藍德紐可亞斯特爾 武朋享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Reed
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname from Middle English and Older Scots red(e) ‘red’, no doubt denoting someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion.

English: from Middle English ride, rede, rude (Old English rīed, rēod, rȳd) ‘clearing’. The surname may be topographic for someone who lived in or near a clearing, or habitational, for someone who lived at one of a number of places so named, including Rede Court in Strood (Kent), Rides in Eastchurch (Kent), Ride Way in Ewhurst (Surrey), and Reed Farm in Wadhurst (Sussex). The word is particularly common in the southeastern counties of England, from Kent to the Isle of Wight. See also Rider and Reader .

English: habitational name from Read (Lancashire), Reed (Hertfordshire), or Rede (Suffolk). The Lancashire placename derives from Old English rǣge ‘roe, female roe deer’ + hēafod ‘head’. The Hertfordshire placename derives from Old English rȳhth ‘rough piece of ground’. The etymology of the Suffolk placename is uncertain.

variants.name: Read
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Reed  .

History: A family called Read were established in America in the early 18th century by John Read, who was born in Dublin, sixth in descent from Sir Thomas Read of Berkshire, England. His son, George Read (1733–98), was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence, and as a lawyer helped frame the Constitution.

variants.name: Ried
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Friedrich, Johann.
German: topographic name from Middle High German riet ‘damp, mossy area, wet land’ or ‘reed grass, swamp’.
German: habitational name from any of various places so named in Hesse, Baden-Württemberg, Austria, Switzerland.

variants.name: Reede
meanings: English: variant of   Reed  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 423, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

variants.name: Reeder
meanings: English: occupational name for someone who used reeds as a roofing material (a thatcher), a derivative of Middle English rede ‘reed’ + -er. Compare Reed and Redman .

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a clearing, a derivative of Middle English rede, ride, rude ‘clearing’ (see Reed ) + -er, synonymous and interchangeable with atte Rede, atte Rude ‘at the clearing’. This type of name, composed of a topographic term + -er, is common in Kent, Surrey and Sussex, and it is also found in Essex.
Dutch and German: variant of   Reder   3.

variants.name: Reeds
meanings: English: variant of Reed , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Reetz   or   Rietz  .

variants.name: Redd
meanings: English: variant of   Reed  .

variants.name: Reid
meanings: Scottish: variant of Reed , of which Reid is the common modern Scots spelling.

History: Samuel Chester Reid (1783–1861), the sea captain who designed the stars and stripes form of the present the American flag, was born at Norwich, CT. His father, Lt. John Reid, a former British naval officer of a distinguished Glasgow family, had resigned his commission and joined the cause of the American Revolution.

variants.name: Roy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Normand, Andre, Jacques, Marcel, Emile, Laurent, Pierre, Cecile, Donat, Gilles.  Indian Dipak, Sumit, Amit, Ajit, Dipankar, Partha, Ranjan, Shyamal, Aloke, Amitava, Chitra, Gautam.
Scottish: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Gaelic ruadh ‘red’. Compare Reed .
English (of Norman origin): from Old French roi ‘king’, used as a nickname (see 3 below) and also as a personal name. Compare Ray 2.

variants.name: Rider
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English ridere ‘horseman’, perhaps with a specific application such as ‘forest ranger’, ‘horseman who guards crops’, or ‘mounted warrior, knight’. Compare Knight .
English: habitational name from Ryther, near Selby (Yorkshire), probably from Old English ryther ‘clearing’.

English: topographic name from Middle English ridere ‘dweller at a clearing’ (see Reeder ) or ‘dweller by a small stream’, depending on which sense of Middle English ride the surname is derived from. These topographic senses of Rider are limited to the southern counties of England (especially Surrey, Sussex, and Hampshire), where topographic names in -er and the Middle English words ride ‘clearing’ and rithe ‘small stream’ are mainly found. See also Reed .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: William Reed was born about 1587, in England. He married Mabel Kendall in 1628, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He immigrated to Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1635 and lived in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1647 and Woburn, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1648. He died on 31 October 1656, in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: William Reed est né environ 1587, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Mabel Kendall en 1628, à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il a immigré au Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1635 et a vécu à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1647 et à Woburn, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1648. Il est décédé le 31 octobre 1656, à Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William Reed wurde um 1587 in England geboren. Er hat 1628 in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England Mabel Kendall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er ist 1635 nach Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America eingewandert. 1647 hat er in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und 1648 in Woburn, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. Er starb am 31. Oktober 1656 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: William Reed è nato il circa 1587 nel England. Lui sposato Mabel Kendall nel 1628, nel Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Immigrato nel Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1635 e vissuto a Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1647 e nel Woburn, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1648. Morì il 31 ottobre 1656, nel Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Reedは約1587年にイギリスで生まれた。1628年にイギリスノースウンバーランドニューカストル ウプオン トインでMabel Kendallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1635年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーボストンに移住し，1647年にはイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーに，1648年にはイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスウオブルンに住んでいた。彼は1656年10月31日にイギリスノースウンバーランドニューカストル ウプオン トインで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Reed(은)는 대략 1587년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1628년 영국 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 Mabel Kendall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1635년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 보스턴로 이민을 떠났고, 1647년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서, 1648년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 보번에서 살았다. 그는 1656년 10월 31일 영국 노르스벌랜드 뉴카스틀 업 타이니에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Reed nasceu aproximadamente 1587, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mabel Kendall em 1628, em Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1635 e viveu em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1647 e em Woburn, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1648. Ele faleceu em 31 de outubro de 1656, em Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William Reed родился около 1587 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mabel Kendall в 1628 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он иммигрировал в бостон, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1635 году, а также жил в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1647 году и в местности вобурн, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1648 году. Он умер 31 октября 1656 г. в местности нюкастл упон тин, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William Reed nació aproximadamente 1587, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Mabel Kendall en 1628, en Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Inmigró a Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1635 y vivió en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1647 y en Woburn, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1648. Murió el 31 de octubre de 1656, en Newcastle upon Tyne, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años.
